credentials.description: AWS access key ID — must have permissions to create EC2, VPC, IAM, CloudTrail, GuardDuty, S3, and Secrets Manager resources

pulumi_config.description: AWS account ID of the adversary (attacker-controlled) account that DangerDev pivots into during the credential abuse chain
pulumi_config.how_to_get: Run: aws sts get-caller-identity --query Account --output text (in the adversary account)

attack_env_vars.description: Passphrase for Pulumi stack encryption — must match the value used during pulumi up
attack_env_vars.how_to_get: Set any secure passphrase before running pulumi up; reuse the same value here

attack_env_vars.description: AWS access key ID for the lab operator IAM user (victim credentials provisioned by pulumi up)
AWS secret access key for the lab operator IAM user
Access key ID of the leaked IAM key embedded in the terraform.tfstate bait file — resolved automatically from stack outputs
Secret access key of the leaked IAM key — resolved automatically from stack outputs
AWS account ID — resolved automatically via sts:GetCallerIdentity if not set
attack_env_vars.how_to_get: Run: aws sts get-caller-identity --query Account --output text
